channel.title: Home on HalfTimeOfLife
channel.description: Recent content in Home on HalfTimeOfLife

item.title: GhidraMAT
item.author: ec.charbonnier@gmail.com (Elouan Charbonnier)
item.description: Ghidra script framework for automated static detection of malware behaviors: anti-debug, anti-VM, packing, C2 indicators, process injection, persistence and defense impairment.

item.title: Malauto
item.author: ec.charbonnier@gmail.com (Elouan Charbonnier)
item.description: WinDbg extension for automated Windows malware analysis. Sets breakpoints, dumps memory regions in PE format and generates structured reports.

item.title: Anti-Emulation Techniques on Windows
item.author: ec.charbonnier@gmail.com (Elouan Charbonnier)
item.description: Article explaining the main emulation detection techniques used by Windows malware, along with bypass strategies.

item.title: Thesis – Detection of Anti-VM Protections Used by Malware
item.author: ec.charbonnier@gmail.com (Elouan Charbonnier)
item.description: In-depth research on anti-emulation techniques and their bypass in the context of Time Travel Debugging (TTD) in full system emulation.

item.title: WINE Malware Analysis – APT29 (2025)
item.author: ec.charbonnier@gmail.com (Elouan Charbonnier)
item.description: Analysis of the WINE malware attributed to APT29 in 2025, including a proof of concept.

item.title: Panoptiv
item.author: ec.charbonnier@gmail.com (Elouan Charbonnier)
item.description: DLL using hooking techniques to bypass anti-VM protections.

item.title: Introduction to Anti-VM Protections
item.author: ec.charbonnier@gmail.com (Elouan Charbonnier)
item.description: Educational video series presenting virtualization detection mechanisms and their implications in malware analysis.

item.title: Linux Kernel Rootkit in 2025
item.author: ec.charbonnier@gmail.com (Elouan Charbonnier)
item.description: Study of the state of Linux rootkits in 2025 through the example of the KoviD rootkit. Analysis of stealth, persistence and kernel security bypass techniques.

item.title: Anonymous Database Query Protocol
item.author: ec.charbonnier@gmail.com (Elouan Charbonnier)
item.description: Implementation of a PIR (Private Information Retrieval) protocol in C, based on Paillier homomorphic encryption to enable anonymous database querying.
